title: Daily Market Digest: July 12, 2026
subtitle: Market closes out week strong on AI enthusiasm ahead of key economic data

items.headline: AI Optimism Fuels U.S. Stocks to Fourth Winning Week; Fed Maintains Hawkish Stance
items.summary: U.S. major indices concluded the week on a positive note, largely driven by sustained enthusiasm for artificial intelligence and technology shares. Despite rising hawkish sentiment from the Federal Reserve, investor confidence in corporate profitability, particularly in the tech sector, continues to propel the market. Economic reports and upcoming earnings will set the tone for the coming week. Not financial advice.

sections.title: Major Indices (As of Friday's Close, July 10, 2026)
Top Movers (As of Friday's Close, July 10, 2026)

items.headline: SK Hynix Soars in U.S. Debut
items.summary: South Korean memory chip giant SK Hynix jumped significantly in its U.S. market debut, reflecting strong investor appetite for AI-linked companies.

items.headline: Nvidia Continues AI-Driven Rally
items.summary: Nvidia remained a strong performer, contributing significantly to the S&P 500's gains amid continued enthusiasm for artificial intelligence.

items.headline: WD-40 Reports Stronger-Than-Expected Profit
items.summary: WD-40's stock jumped after the company reported much stronger profit for its latest quarter than analysts had anticipated.

items.headline: IBM Among Friday's Decliners
items.summary: IBM was one of the notable decliners during Friday's trading session.

items.headline: UnitedHealth Faces Sell-Off
items.summary: UnitedHealth saw a decline in its share price by the close of Friday's trading.

items.headline: Merck Shares Dip
items.summary: Merck's stock closed lower on Friday, contributing to the day's market losers.

items.headline: Technology and AI Continue to Lead Market Sectors
items.summary: AI and technology-linked companies remained the primary drivers of market support this past week, reinforcing a broader trend seen throughout the year. Information Technology notably outperformed the overall S&P 500 in the second quarter.

items.headline: Federal Reserve Leans Hawkish Amid Inflation Concerns
items.summary: The Federal Reserve's stance has become increasingly hawkish, with many FOMC members signaling potential interest rate hikes in 2026. This comes as inflation remains elevated relative to the Committee's 2 percent goal. The target range for the federal funds rate has been maintained at 3½ to 3¾ percent since the start of the year.

items.headline: Key Economic Reports Expected Next Week
items.summary: Investors are closely watching for the release of the U.S. June Consumer Price Index (CPI) and Producer Price Index (PPI) reports next week, which will provide further insights into inflation trends. Additionally, Federal Reserve Chairman Kevin Warsh is scheduled to testify before Congress.

items.headline: Q2 Earnings Season Commences with Major Reports
items.summary: The second-quarter earnings season has officially begun, with major companies like JPMorgan, Netflix, and TSMC slated to report. Delta Air Lines already announced Q2 results that surpassed expectations, with earnings per share at $1.56.

items.headline: Apple Files Lawsuit Against OpenAI Over IP Theft Allegations
items.summary: Apple Inc. has reportedly filed a lawsuit against OpenAI, accusing the ChatGPT owner and its hardware chief of stealing confidential designs and prototypes. This news comes as Apple's stock saw a slight dip.

items.headline: Commodities and Currencies: Gold Dips, Bitcoin Gains
items.summary: In the broader market, the 10-year U.S. government bond yield stood at 4.56%. Gold declined by 0.77% to $4,120.20, while oil slipped slightly by 0.03% to $71.62 per barrel. Bitcoin, however, saw a gain of 1.71%, reaching $63,899.14.
